onboarding.question:
1. Specify the exact placement of the "Not signed in" indicator (e.g., top bar, bottom bar, fixed region) and confirm it does not overlay critical UI elements like the message input or AI responses.
2. Define the total unauthenticated session length and clarify that the 10-minute warning in AC#8 fires when 10 minutes of inactivity remain before the 60-minute session expires.
3. Define what "complete AI response" means operationally for triggering the in-conversation nudge (e.g., final streaming token received, response fully rendered, or first user message received after the AI's first response is visible).
onboarding.recommendations:
[1]. My recommendation: The "Not signed in" indicator is placed in a fixed top bar region above the chat interface, occupying a single-line horizontal band that does not overlay the message input area, conversation thread, or AI responses; it remains visible at all times during logged-out sessions and is positioned similarly across all surfaces (mobile, tablet, desktop).

→ Rationale: A fixed top bar is a standard, predictable placement that does not interfere with core interaction areas; it provides persistent visibility without competing for space with conversational content; this pattern is familiar across mobile and web applications.

→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

---

[2]. My recommendation: The unauthenticated session length is 60 minutes of inactivity (per AC#23); the 10-minute warning in AC#8 is triggered when the inactivity timer has 10 minutes remaining before session expiry (i.e., when 50 minutes of the 60-minute window have elapsed without user action).

→ Rationale: This is already defined in AC#23 and AC#8; confirming it explicitly removes ambiguity about the warning trigger timing and ensures design and engineering have identical understanding of the countdown.

→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

---

[3]. My recommendation: "Complete AI response" means the final streaming token has been received and rendered in the chat interface — the AI's response is fully visible to the user and the response stream has ended; the in-conversation nudge fires on the next opportunity after this point (e.g., immediately after the response is complete, or as the user's first opportunity to send a follow-up message).

→ Rationale: Defining completion by final token arrival and rendering gives an observable, implementable trigger; it ensures the nudge appears only after the user has experienced a meaningful AI interaction, not during mid-stream responses; this respects the product intent that the nudge surfaces after demonstrating value.

→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

---

Say *yes* to apply these to the product spec and continue design, or reply to adjust any recommendation.
